name: Paanagam with Dry Ginger
description: Paanagam with Dry Ginger is a spiced variation of the traditional South Indian jaggery drink, specially prepared during Sri Rama Navami and other summer festivals. The addition of dry ginger powder (sukku) gives it a warm, digestive kick that balances the cooling properties of jaggery and lemon. It is aromatic, refreshing and deeply rooted in South Indian temple tradition as a sacred prasadam.

steps.instruction: Using a hand mortar and pestle, crush 10 peppercorns and 2 cardamom pods to a coarse mixture. Set aside.
steps.tip: Freshly crushed pepper and cardamom give more flavour than pre-ground powders.

steps.instruction: In a mixing bowl, add 2½ cups water and ½ cup powdered jaggery.
steps.tip: Use powdered jaggery for quick dissolving. If using block jaggery, grate it first.

steps.instruction: Mix well until the jaggery dissolves completely.
Add the crushed pepper and cardamom mixture.
Add ¼ tsp dry ginger powder, a tiny pinch of edible camphor and a pinch of salt.
steps.tip: Do not add more edible camphor than a tiny pinch — it can overpower the drink.

steps.instruction: Add lemon juice from ½ lemon.
steps.tip: Adjust lemon juice according to your taste.

steps.instruction: Add a few tulsi leaves if desired.
Mix everything well. Let it rest for at least a few minutes for the flavours to blend.
steps.tip: Let the paanagam rest for a few minutes before straining — this allows all spices to infuse.

steps.instruction: Serve chilled or at room temperature.
steps.tip: Paanagam keeps well in room temperature for 1 day and in the fridge for 2 days.

tips: Use powdered jaggery for quick dissolving. If using block jaggery, grate it first.
Freshly crushed pepper and cardamom give more flavour than pre-ground powders.
Let the paanagam rest for a few minutes before straining — this allows all spices to infuse.
Adjust lemon juice according to your taste.
Use paagu vellam (soft, dark reddish-orange jaggery) for a beautiful golden-brown colour.
Do not add more edible camphor than a tiny pinch — it can overpower the drink.
Paanagam keeps well in room temperature for 1 day and in the fridge for 2 days.
The dry ginger adds warmth, making this version especially good during slightly cooler days too.
